name: Päijänne Tavastia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the Päijänne Tavastia arms, evoking Lake Päijänne
the golden mermaid and cuckoo of the arms

description: A blue banner-format flag charged with the golden arms of Päijänne Tavastia: a mermaid, with a cuckoo in the upper hoist, all in gold on the blue field.
history: Päijänne Tavastia (Päijät-Häme) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform. Its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, whose mermaid (the water-goddess Vellamo) and cuckoo refer to Lake Päijänne and the region's long ridges.

features.symbolism: Lake Päijänne and the waters of the region

features.role: a golden cuckoo in the dexter chief (upper hoist)
features.symbolism: the forests and ridges of Päijänne Tavastia
